vulnerabilities.title: Exposed AWS Access Key
vulnerabilities.description: A hardcoded AWS Access Key ID was detected. This allows unauthorized access to AWS resources.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the hardcoded key and use environment variables (e.g., process.env.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID). Rotate the key immediately in the AWS console.

vulnerabilities.title: Remote Code Execution in 'lodash'
vulnerabilities.description: The installed version of lodash (4.17.15) is vulnerable to Prototype Pollution which can lead to Remote Code Execution.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Update lodash to version 4.17.21 or higher.

vulnerabilities.title: SQL Injection vulnerability
vulnerabilities.description: User input is concatenated directly into a SQL query without parameterization.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Use prepared statements or parameterized queries provided by your ORM/database driver.

vulnerabilities.title: Missing Security Headers
vulnerabilities.description: The application does not enforce Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS) or X-Content-Type-Options.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Implement a security middleware like 'helmet' in Express to automatically set recommended HTTP headers.

vulnerabilities.title: Insecure JWT Secret
vulnerabilities.description: The JWT signing secret is too short or predictable (e.g., 'secret123').
vulnerabilities.remediation: Generate a strong, random 256-bit key and store it securely in environment variables.
